description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, dark brown stone block with a coarse, granular texture and irregular, chipped edges. The surface appears uneven, with some areas flatter and others more jagged, suggesting it has been broken or cut from a larger piece. One side features a white patch with handwritten markings, possibly for cataloging or identification, and a faint barcode. The coloration is mostly a deep brown with some lighter speckles, indicating a natural stone composition. The block is solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings, and its surfaces show minor signs of wear but no significant weathering. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a barrier or boundary element in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A dense, dark brown stone block with a rough, granular texture and irregular, chipped edges. One side has a white patch with catalog markings.
description_view_0: The top and one long side of the stone block are visible, showing a flat, rough surface with granular texture and uneven edges.
description_view_1: This view highlights a short, jagged end of the block, revealing its thickness and the irregularity of its break.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is shown, featuring a white patch with handwritten markings and a barcode, contrasting with the dark stone.
description_view_3: Another short end is visible, similar to view 1, with a focus on the block's depth and rough, chipped surface.
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor surface wear and chipping on edges, but no significant weathering or moss growth visible.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Handwritten catalog markings and a barcode on one side.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Isolated or loosely aligned with other blocks
